## WORKFLOW COORDINATOR SYNTHESIS — RANKED ISSUES FOR EXEC REVIEW

PUSH-001 (P0) is confirmed fixed. Four new issues were found in the surrounding push and paywall infrastructure, ranging from a silent broken import (P1) to a duplicate-notification race (P2). All prior carry-forward items are unchanged. No syntax or parse errors across the full source tree.

## SECTION 2 — RESOLVED ITEMS (CONFIRMED)

### ✅ PUSH-001 — Push dispatcher now uses Expo Push API (RESOLVED)

**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/cron/push-dispatcher.js` (commit `1879c5b`)

The dispatcher has been correctly rewritten to use `https://exp.host/--/api/v2/push/send`. Verification points:

- `sendExpoPush()` constructs a message object with `to: expoToken` and wraps it in an array (`[message]`) per Expo's spec.
- HTTP response handling checks `result.errors` (request-level failures) and `result.data[0].status` (per-message ticket).
- Stale-token cleanup now triggers on `DeviceNotRegistered` (Expo's term), while retaining `NotRegistered` and `InvalidRegistration` for backward compatibility with any legacy rows.
- `FCM_SERVER_KEY` dependency removed. `EXPO_ACCESS_TOKEN` is optional and correctly attached as a Bearer header when present.
- The `FCM_SERVER_KEY` early-exit guard that was previously hiding the real bug is gone — the dispatcher now runs unconditionally.

The fix is server-only (no mobile changes required). This was the correct choice (Option A). **Not regressed.**

---

## SECTION 3 — OPEN ISSUES (CARRY-FORWARD)

### ❌ P0-003 — HealthKit `requestHealthKitPermissions` is a stub (UNCHANGED)

**File:** `mobile/src/services/healthKit.ts` (lines 66–91)

No change. `requestHealthKitPermissions()` returns `false` immediately. `react-native-health` import is commented out. The real implementation is fully documented in the TODO comment — this is purely a build dependency gap. Requires EAS build + Apple device. The `NSHealthShareUsageDescription` entitlement is already in `app.json`; Apple reviewers will verify it functions. **Pre-App Store submission blocker.**

### ⚠️ BUG-008 — `confirmedThisSession` still in-memory only (PARTIALLY MITIGATED, P3)

**File:** `mobile/app/(tabs)/rankings.tsx` (line 589)

State is `useState<Set<string>>(() => new Set())`. The partial fix (`|| ranking.confirmed_1rm_kg != null`) covers the post-batch restart case. Residual gap: user confirms, restarts before nightly cron batch runs, CTA reappears. One-line AsyncStorage write eliminates it. Not a launch blocker.

### ⚠️ CSV-003 — Strava average speed unit unconfirmed (PARTIALLY MITIGATED, P2)

**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/routes/csvImport.js` (lines 47–72)

The `1000 / speed` formula (for m/s → sec/km) has a 120 sec/km sanity clamp to handle the km/h ambiguity. No real Strava export has been used to confirm the unit. No corrupt data can be written due to the clamp. A 60-second action from anyone with a Strava metric account unblocks this definitively.

---

## SECTION 4 — NEW ISSUES

### 🟠 NEW-001 — `registerForPushNotifications` import name mismatch in `AuthContext.tsx` — silent dead code

**Severity:** P1
**Files:** `mobile/src/context/AuthContext.tsx` (line 65, 305), `mobile/src/services/pushNotifications.ts` (line 38)

**Evidence:**

`AuthContext.tsx` imports a named export that does not exist:
```typescript
// AuthContext.tsx line 65
import { registerForPushNotifications } from '../services/pushNotifications';
```

The service exports:
```typescript
// pushNotifications.ts line 38
export async function registerForPushNotificationsAsync(): Promise<void> { ... }
```

The import resolves to `undefined` at runtime. When called on line 305 (`const result = await registerForPushNotifications()`), this throws a `TypeError: registerForPushNotifications is not a function`. The call is inside a `try/catch` with a silent swallow, so the failure is invisible.

**Net effect:** The `AuthContext` push registration path is completely non-functional. It has never worked.

**Why push notifications still work:** `_layout.tsx` independently calls `registerForPushNotificationsAsync()` (correctly named) after auth resolves. That path correctly stores the token via `patchProfile({ fcm_token: token.data })`. The `AuthContext` path is duplicative dead code — but its brokenness means any future developer relying on it (e.g., for post-login re-registration) will find it silently failing.

**Also in `AuthContext`:** Line 308 calls `registerPushToken({ token, platform })` which POSTs to `/user/push-token` — an endpoint that **does not exist** in `user.js` (confirmed by code review). `pushTokens.ts` itself documents this in a `TODO` comment. This call will 404 silently.

**Fix:** Either (a) delete the entire `_registerPushToken` callback in `AuthContext` (since `_layout.tsx` already handles registration correctly), or (b) rename the import to `registerForPushNotificationsAsync` and align the API path. Option (a) is lower risk and removes the dead code.

---

### 🟡 NEW-002 — Double paywall push notification on session 5 — two independent code paths both enqueue

**Severity:** P2
**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/routes/workouts.js` (lines 64–135)

**Evidence:**

On `POST /workouts`, when a new session is inserted, two separate paywall paths both execute and both can enqueue a push notification for the same user on the same request:

**Path A** (synchronous, pre-response, lines 63–93):
```js
if (wasInserted && userData?.tier === 'free' && sessionCount >= FREE_SESSION_LIMIT) {
    supabaseAdmin.from('notification_queue').insert({
        type: 'paywall_session_limit',
        title: 'Upgrade to Peak Fettle Pro',
        body: `You've logged ${sessionCount} sessions — ...`,
    })
}
```

**Path B** (async fire-and-forget, post-response, lines 97–135):
```js
if (!userRow?.paywall_triggered_at && count >= FREE_SESSION_LIMIT) {
    // marks paywall_triggered_at, then:
    pool.query(`INSERT INTO notification_queue (...) VALUES ($1, 'paywall_trigger', 'You''re on a roll! 🔥', ...)`)
}
```

Path B checks `paywall_triggered_at` before inserting — but Path A runs (and enqueues) **before** Path B sets `paywall_triggered_at`. On the 5th session, both conditions are true simultaneously, both enqueue, and the user receives two push notifications with different titles and bodies for the same paywall event.

**Additional issue:** Path A uses `countRealSessions()` (filters `session_type IN ('lift', NULL)` and `deleted_at IS NULL` — correct). Path B uses `COUNT(*) FROM workouts` (counts all rows including rest days, cardio imports, and soft-deleted rows — incorrect). These can disagree on when the threshold is crossed. A user with 3 real lifts + 2 rest days would trigger Path B but not Path A.

**Fix:** Remove Path A's push enqueue (leave the `paywallTrigger` flag for the in-response signal, which is the primary mechanism). Rely solely on Path B for the push notification. Fix Path B's `COUNT(*)` to use the same `countRealSessions` logic.

---

### 🟡 NEW-003 — `notification_queue` has no retry cap — persistently-failed notifications retry forever

**Severity:** P2
**Files:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/cron/push-dispatcher.js`, `peak-fettle-agents/server/migrations/20260517_notification_queue.sql`

**Evidence:**

The `notification_queue` schema has `sent_at` (NULL = pending) and `error` (last error string) but no `retry_count` or `failed_permanently` column:

```sql
CREATE TABLE notification_queue (
    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    sent_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,        -- NULL = pending
    error       TEXT,               -- last dispatch error
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
    -- NO retry_count, NO max_retries, NO failed_at
);
```

The dispatcher's retry logic: a notification with `sent_at IS NULL` is retried every 5 minutes, unconditionally, regardless of how many times it has failed. A notification that fails for a non-`DeviceNotRegistered` reason (e.g., `MessageTooBig`, `MismatchSenderId`, a temporary Expo outage mis-classified as permanent, or a corrupt `data` payload) will be retried forever.

**Impact at current scale:** Low — the queue is small. At launch scale with hundreds of users, a batch of undeliverable messages will churn every 5 minutes, consuming DB connections and Expo API quota.

**Fix:** Add a `retry_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` and `failed_permanently BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` column via migration. Increment on each failure; mark `failed_permanently = TRUE` after 5 retries (or immediately for `DeviceNotRegistered`). Filter `WHERE NOT failed_permanently` in the dispatcher query.

---

### 🟡 NEW-004 — Expo Push API called one-message-per-request instead of batching — 50× API overhead at scale

**Severity:** P2 (performance, not correctness)
**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/cron/push-dispatcher.js` (line 84)

**Evidence:**

Each notification is sent as a separate HTTP request:
```js
body: JSON.stringify([message]),  // array of 1
```

The Expo Push API accepts up to **100 messages per request** in a single batch. With `BATCH_SIZE = 50`, the current implementation makes 50 sequential HTTP round-trips where 1 would suffice.

**Impact now:** Negligible — the queue is small and the 5-minute cron schedule absorbs the latency. **Impact at launch scale:** A group-streak event (e.g., all members of 50 active groups miss a workout) could enqueue 150+ notifications simultaneously. At 50 sequential HTTP calls per batch, multiple cron cycles would be needed to drain the queue, delaying time-sensitive streak alerts.

**Fix:** Collect all `pending` rows, chunk into groups of 100, send each chunk as a single request (`body: JSON.stringify(chunk)`), then map ticket responses back to row IDs for the update step. The ticket array is positionally aligned with the message array, so the mapping is straightforward.

---

### 🟢 NEW-005 — AI plan generation has no request timeout — hangs indefinitely if Claude Haiku is unresponsive

**Severity:** P3
**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/routes/plans.js` (line 455)

**Evidence:**

```js
const message = await anthropic.messages.create({
    model:      'claude-haiku-4-5',
    max_tokens: 1024,
    system:     systemPrompt,
    messages:   [{ role: 'user', content: userMessage }],
    // NO timeout, NO AbortController, NO Promise.race
});
```

If the Anthropic API is slow or unresponsive, this `await` has no upper time bound. The Express request will hang until the client's own timeout fires (or indefinitely if the client is also waiting patiently). Under a sustained API degradation, worker threads accumulate and the server becomes unresponsive.

**Fix:** Wrap in a `Promise.race` against a `setTimeout` rejection (e.g., 30 seconds), or pass an `AbortSignal` via the Anthropic SDK's `signal` option. Return a 504 to the client with a human-readable retry message.

## EXEC SUMMARY — RANKED PRIORITY LIST (WORKFLOW COORDINATOR)

**Rank 1 — P0-003 HealthKit stub (🔴 P0 — pre-App Store submission)**
`requestHealthKitPermissions()` is a no-op. Every iOS user who grants HealthKit on the onboarding screen gets nothing back. Apple reviewers will exercise the `NSHealthShareUsageDescription` entitlement that is already declared in `app.json` — if it does nothing, the app will be rejected. Requires `react-native-health` install + EAS dev build. Gating behind a feature flag buys time but does not eliminate the App Store risk.

**Rank 2 — NEW-001 `registerForPushNotifications` name mismatch (🟠 P1 — fix before next push infrastructure work)**
The AuthContext push registration path silently does nothing. The real push path (`_layout.tsx`) works, so push notifications are not broken today — but the broken AuthContext code will trap any developer who extends it, and the missing `/user/push-token` endpoint means any future attempt to migrate to that path will 404. Clean up now while the context is fresh: remove the dead `_registerPushToken` callback from `AuthContext` and the dead `pushTokens.ts` module, or promote the `_layout.tsx` approach as the canonical path explicitly.

**Rank 3 — NEW-002 Double paywall notification (🟡 P2 — fix before public launch)**
Free-tier users hitting session 5 receive two push notifications for the same event, with different titles. This is a user-experience defect visible to every user who converts from free to paid. Path B's incorrect `COUNT(*)` also means it can trigger on session counts that don't represent real workouts. Fix is small: remove Path A's push enqueue, fix Path B's count query.

**Rank 4 — NEW-003 No retry cap on `notification_queue` (🟡 P2 — fix before significant user volume)**
Undeliverable notifications retry every 5 minutes forever. Not urgent at current user count; becomes a queue-health and API-quota problem at hundreds of active users. One migration + two dispatcher changes.

**Rank 5 — NEW-004 Expo Push API not batching (🟡 P2 — fix before group-streak launch)**
50 HTTP round-trips per cron run is harmless now, but one group-streak event can produce 100+ queued notifications. Fix before TICKET-025 (Group Streak Credits) goes live; that feature is the most likely trigger of large simultaneous batches.

**Rank 6 — NEW-005 AI plan generation no timeout (🟢 P3 — low urgency, add before prod traffic)**
Anthropic API calls have no timeout guard. Low risk in beta with a small user base and a responsive API. Add a 30-second `Promise.race` before increasing AI plan generation traffic.

**Rank 7 — EAS Build (🔴 INFRA — highest-leverage infrastructure action)**
Unchanged. EAS Build unlocks all device verification, TICKET-025 sign-off, TICKET-027 offline sync testing, HealthKit unblocking, and App Store submission.

**Rank 8 — BUG-008 / CSV-003 (🟢 P3 / 🟡 P2 — carry-forward, unchanged priority)**
BUG-008 is post-launch if needed. CSV-003 needs a real Strava export to close — one test file from any team member unblocks it.
